channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Turkey calls for reconciliation between Syria government and opposition to end conflict
item.description: ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — The recent rapid advance by opposition fighters in Syria shows that Syrian President Bashar Assad must reconcile with his own people and hold dialogue with the opposition, the Turkish foreign minister said Monday.
item.dc:creator: SUZAN FRASER and KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump warns of 'hell to pay' if Hamas doesn't release hostages
item.description: President-elect Trump on Monday threatened to unleash “hell” in the Middle East if hostages held by Hamas in the Gaza Strip are not released before his inauguration Jan.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Panic among spectators at soccer game kills at least 56 in the West African nation of Guinea
item.description: CONAKRY, Guinea (AP) — Chaos erupted at a soccer game in Guinea after fans protested a referee’s call and thousands of panicked spectators tried to flee the stadium, leaving at least 56 people dead in the West African nation, officials and witnesses said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: IDF soldiers accuse UN peacekeepers of enabling Hezbollah terrorists amid increasing cease-fire violations
item.description: In southern Lebanon, Israeli forces confront entrenched Hezbollah positions and question UNIFIL’s ability to enforce its mandate even before the cease-fire agreement was drawn up.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden administration to announce $725M weapons package to Ukraine months before Trump is sworn in
item.description: The Biden administration is announcing a $725 million weapons package for Ukraine less than two months before President-elect Donald Trump takes office, a U.S. official says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A small migrant caravan sets out from southern Mexico but it's unlikely to reach the US border
item.description: A small migrant caravan has set out from southern Mexico, heading north, but is unlikely to reach the U.S. border after authorities broke up two other small caravans headed to the United States over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Edgar H. Clemente, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Georgia's protests lay bare a clash of values and interests as it navigates a Russia-West standoff
item.description: TBILISI, Georgia (AP) — Georgia is convulsed by political upheaval again after its pro-Moscow government, fresh off parliamentary elections denounced as rigged by its critics, decided to suspend negotiations for the small former Soviet republic to join the European Union.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Taiwan president speaks with US officials from Hawaii as China condemns visit
item.description: Taiwanese President Lai Ching-te touched down in Hawaii over the weekend and spoke by phone with former Speaker Nancy Pelosi (D-Calif.) as China slammed the U.S. for the visit and for selling weapons parts to Taipei. Hawaii Gov.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Hezbollah fires into Israel-held area after multiple Israeli strikes in Lebanon since truce began
item.description: Hezbollah fired into a disputed border zone held by Israel after multiple Israeli strikes inside Lebanon since a ceasefire took hold last week.
item.dc:creator: Natalie Meltzer And Sally Abou Aljoud, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US Navy ships repel attack from Houthis in Gulf of Aden
item.description: The U.S. Navy destroyers USS Stockdale and USS O’Kane repelled an attack against American ships by Yemen-based Houthi rebels, CENTCOM says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Landmark climate change case opens at the top UN court as island nations fear rising seas
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — The top United Nations court took up the largest case in its history on Monday, hearing the plight of several small island nations helpless in combating the devastating impact of climate change that they feel endangers their very survival.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Volkswagen workers hold strikes to push back against proposed pay cuts
item.description: Volkswagen workers launched rolling strikes at nine plants across Germany to underscore their resistance to pay cuts
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hong Kong launches panda sculpture tour as the city hopes the bear craze boosts tourism
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — Thousands of giant panda sculptures will greet residents and tourists starting Saturday in Hong Kong, where enthusiasm for the bears has grown since two cubs were born in a local theme park.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Impeachment complaint filed against Philippine Vice President Duterte after she threatened president
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — An impeachment complaint was filed Monday against Philippine Vice President Sara Duterte, who is facing a legal storm over a death threat she made against the president and her alleged role in extra-judicial killings of drug suspects, corruption and failure to stand up to
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel and Hezbollah trade fire, threatening Lebanon ceasefire
item.description: The Israeli military says Hezbollah has fired into a disputed border zone, its first attack since a ceasefire took hold last week.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Panic among spectators at soccer game kills at least 56 in the West African nation of Guinea
item.description: Officials and witnesses say chaos erupted at a soccer game in Guinea after fans protested a referee’s call and thousands of panicked spectators tried to flee the stadium, leaving at least 56 people dead in the West African nation.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The International Criminal Court's chief lashes out at threats from Russia and a US senator
item.description: The president of the International Criminal Court has lashed out at Russia for targeting its prosecutors and judges over the panel’s investigation of Russian President Vladimir Putin, and slammed comments by a U.S. senator threatening the court.
item.dc:creator: Molly Quell, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Over 40 people hospitalized in Georgia during protests over the suspension of EU talks
item.description: TBILISI, Georgia (AP) — A third night of protests in the Georgian capital against the government’s decision to suspend negotiations to join the European Union left 44 people hospitalized, officials said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIKO MEGRELIDZE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Syria launches counterattacks in an attempt to halt insurgency, as Iran's top diplomat meets Assad
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — The Syrian military rushed reinforcements to the northwest and launched airstrikes Sunday in an attempt to push back insurgents who seized the country’s largest city of Aleppo, as Iran pledged to help the government counter the surprise offensive.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Taiwan's president visits East-West think tank as China criticizes his 2-day visit to Hawaii
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — Taiwan's president visited a U.S. State Department-funded think tank and educational institution Sunday on the second day of a two-day visit to Hawaii that's part of a Pacific island tour that has already triggered criticism from Beijing.
item.dc:creator: AUDREY McAVOY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bangladesh court acquits Zia’s son and 48 others convicted in 2004 deadly grenade attack
item.description: DHAKA, Bangladesh (AP) — Bangladesh’s High Court on Sunday acquitted former Prime Minister Khaleda Zia’s son, Tarique Rahman, and 48 others, overturning their verdicts in a deadly 2004 grenade attack on a political rally.
item.dc:creator: JULHAS ALAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Social democrats gain and incumbents are punished in Iceland's election
item.description: REYKJAVIK, Iceland (AP) — Voters in Iceland joined a global trend of punishing incumbents in a parliamentary election, with a center-left party winning the largest share of votes in the North Atlantic island nation.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Plastic treaty talks draw to a close with production limits still under debate
item.description: BUSAN, South Korea (AP) — Negotiations on a treaty to end plastic pollution are drawing to a close Sunday as nations continue debating whether part of that should be putting limits on plastic production.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China sentences journalist for espionage after he was detained while eating with Japanese diplomat
item.description: Chinese journalist Dong Yuyu was sentenced in a Beijing court to seven years in prison for espionage after meeting with a Japanese diplomat at a restaurant.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: After entering Aleppo, Syrian insurgents advance to a nearby province. Assad says he'll defeat them
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Thousands of Syrian insurgents took over most of Aleppo on Saturday, establishing positions in the country's largest city and controlling its airport before expanding their shock offensive to a nearby province.
item.dc:creator: SARAH EL DEEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Islamist rebels in Syria catch Assad, Putin, Iran regimes off guard giving US new mideast headache
item.description: Amid a rapid-fire collapse of Syrian, Hezbollah, Russian and Iranian forces in northern Syria, questions are surfacing about U.S. policy toward the emerging new order in the war-ravaged Syrian Arab Republic.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel hits alleged Hezbollah weapons smuggling sites in Syria
item.description: Israeli military jets hit alleged Hezbollah weapons smuggling sites near the border crossing between Lebanon and Syria, according to officials, who allege the Lebanese militant group violated a ceasefire agreement.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Trump tariffs threaten to crack open North American economies
item.description: MEXICO CITY — President-elect Trump’s pledge to impose tariffs on Mexico and Canada threatens to upend decades of North American integration, a bumpy process that’s recast the two U.S. neighbors as providers of raw and finished goods for the world’s biggest economy.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, The Hill

item.title: Customers at this Starbucks can sip coffee and observe a quiet North Korean village
item.description: Coffee drinkers can sip their beverages and view a quiet North Korean mountain village from a new Starbucks at a South Korean border observatory.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nigeria confirms that at least 27 people died and more than 100 are missing after boat capsized
item.description: ABUJA, Nigeria (AP) — At least 27 people died and more than 100, mostly women, were missing on Friday, after a boat transporting them to a food market capsized along the River Niger in northern Nigeria, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: CHINEDU ASADU, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: School bus driver charged with holding girl captive for 12 years
item.description: A school bus driver in Colombia allegedly held a girl captive for more than a decade until she recently escaped, prosecutors say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Stowaway found on Delta flight from New York City to Paris
item.description: The TSA says the woman bypassed two identity verification areas and boarding status stations before getting on the plane.
item.dc:creator: Xavier Walton, NewsNation

item.title: Biden readies $725 million arms aid package for Ukraine: Reuters
item.description: The formal notification to Congress of the weapons package could come as soon as Monday, one official said.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Biden team briefed Trump transition on Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire
item.description: President-elect Trump's transition team has received briefings from the chief architect of the Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire deal under President Biden, a senior administration official said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: China releases 3 Americans in prisoner swap
item.description: Mark Swidan, Kai Li and John Leung were freed after years of talks between U.S. and Chinese officials.
item.dc:creator: Katie Smith, NewsNation

item.title: Russian migrants being denied access to asylum process: attorney
item.description: "They are being subjected to a blanket policy that they are applying to Russian asylum seekers," he said.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: Amelia Earhart’s plane photo not of the plane, company says
item.description: “After 11 months the waiting has finally ended and unfortunately our target was not Amelia's Electra 10E," the exploration company said.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, NewsNation
